task_completion.description: Navigate to the configuration page of the 'Deploy-Service' job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins configuration page is visible with 'Deploy-Service' in the title or breadcrumb navigation.

task_completion.description: Enable the parameterization option for the job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox for 'This project is parameterized' is checked in the General configuration section.
A parameter configuration block of type 'Choice Parameter' is visible with the 'Name' field set exactly to 'TARGET_ENV'.

task_completion.description: Configure the choices for the 'TARGET_ENV' parameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Choices' text area contains the words 'development', 'staging', and 'production', typically on separate lines.

task_completion.description: Update the build script to dynamically use the environment parameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Execute shell' command box shows an echo command that includes the variable, such as '$TARGET_ENV' or '${TARGET_ENV}', instead of a hardcoded environment name.
The job dashboard for 'Deploy-Service' is visible, and the left sidebar shows a 'Build with Parameters' link, indicating the changes were successfully saved.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Jenkins web UI to configure the job.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jenkins web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands, curl, or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Jenkins application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Jenkins interface is genuine and not a text editor or mock application.

integrity.description: Verify the build script uses a variable, not a hardcoded string.
integrity.visual_evidence: The shell script in the configuration uses a variable like $TARGET_ENV rather than hardcoding 'staging' or 'development'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Job Name: Deploy-Service. Parameter Name: TARGET_ENV. Parameter Choices: development, staging, production. The build script must dynamically use the parameter (e.g., $TARGET_ENV) to output the deployment message.
